channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: How leaders are protecting culture while AI rewrites how work gets done
item.description: At Ingka Group, the largest IKEA retailer in 32 countries, leaders set out to adopt AI in a way that wouldnât put their culture at risk.

item.title: Here's how the DHS shutdown could impact the lives of everyday Americans
item.description: A government shutdown hit the Department of Homeland Security, affecting TSA airport security, FEMA disaster relief, and immigration services nationwi...

item.title: OpenAIâs Codex and Anthropicâs Claude spark coding revolution as developers say theyâve abandoned traditional programming
item.description: New releases from OpenAI and Anthropic sparked an existential crisis among coders, but many engineers say they stopped coding months ago.

item.title: Government shutdown hits DHS after Democrats blow up bipartisan funding deal over immigration uproar
item.description: DHS is in a partial shutdown after Congress failed to reach an agreement on immigration issues in time for its self-imposed deadline.

item.title: 'Fiction': House Republican campaign chair dismisses Democratsâ expanding GOP target map
item.description: House GOP campaign chair scoffs at Democrats' move to expand their midterm battleground map to 44 House districts in this year's battle for the House ...

item.title: Virginia Supreme Court OKs redistricting special election before midterms
item.description: Virginia's Supreme Court greenlit a redistricting referendum for spring, handing Democrats a small win in the nationwide partisan fight to redraw cong...

item.title: The Folly of Switzerlandâs Proposed Population Cap
item.description: Swiss voters are being asked to decide on a referendum to limit the countryâs population to 10 million people.

item.title: The Nixon Library Is Wrong About Nixon And The Deep State
item.description: The new revelation about Nixon 'bears directly on allegations by President Trump and his supporters about the existence of what was once called the pe...

item.title: Marc Andreessen made a dire software prediction 15 years ago. Now itâs happening in a way nobody imagined
item.description: The billionaire venture capitalist explained why software would eat the world in 2011. He had no idea.

item.title: Schumer's 'E. coli' burger photo resurfaces after another Dem's grilling skills get torched: 'What is that?'
item.description: Democratic Gov. Abigail Spanberger is being mocked for a photo of her grilling, with many on social media expressing alarm and disgust over the meat o...

item.title: Judge rules ICE can't make warrantless arrests at some churches
item.description: A federal judge said Friday that ICE's policy of allowing arrests at churches tramples on religious rights, and he issued an order blocking arrests un...

item.title: Biden administration paid millions in a sweetheart deal to care for migrant children
item.description: The Biden administration awarded a bloated half-billion-dollar contract to take care of migrant children to a politically connected nonprofit that had...

item.title: Here Are 10 Great Justice Scalia Quotes To Mark A Decade Since His Passing
item.description: Here are some of Justice Scalia's best moments in remembrance of the 10-year anniversary of his passing.

item.title: Eileen Guâs Terrible Choice
item.description: Gu skis for China, a choice that is a little like deciding to represent a fascist country during the 1930s.

item.title: The Week: The Nancy Guthrie Disappearance
item.description: Plus: Trump rolls back climate regulations.

item.title: AOC at Munich Security Conference accuses Trump of authoritarianism that puts the world at risk
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez trod onto the international stage Friday at the Munich Security Conference, where she condemned America's president as a...

item.title: Turns Out The Irishman Detained By ICE Was Facing Drug Charges Back Home
item.description: The arrest of Irishman Seamus Culleton fueled a false media narrative that ICE is grabbing law-abiding people who have done nothing wrong.

item.title: Mamdami Demands U.S. Immigration Policy Start Obeying Islam
item.description: Mamdani invoked Islamic doctrine to define civic obligation and delegitimize lawful, constitutional authority.

item.title: Virginia High Court Greenlights Democrat Effort To Give Millions Of Republicans A Single Seat In Congress
item.description: The Virginia Supreme Court handed Democrats a massive win Friday, allowing them to move forward with a redistricting referendum that would gerrymander...

item.title: Rubio steps into Munich spotlight as Trump leans on him to carry Vanceâs populist message abroad
item.description: Marco Rubio attends Munich Security Conference following Vice President JD Vance's controversial 2025 speech that stunned European leaders and critici...

item.title: Republicans ask Supreme Court to block pro-Democrat rewrite of N.Y. congressional map
item.description: New York Republicans rushed to the Supreme Court Friday to ask the justices to block a last-minute attempt to redraw a GOP-held congressional district...

item.title: AI and Our Collapsing Creative Horizons
item.description: The real danger AI poses is to original thought itself.

item.title: Bank of America liftsÂ MoynihanâsÂ pay 17% to $41 million for 2025
item.description: A year ago, Moynihanâs compensation was increased 21% to $35 million after the bank was able to boost its earnings.

item.title: Trump says regime change in Iran that ousts Islamic clerics âwould be the best thing that could happenâ as another carrier heads to Mideast
item.description: Trump said the USS Gerald R. Ford, the world's largest aircraft carrier, is being sent from the Caribbean Sea to the Mideast.

item.title: Congress Has Always Held the Power over Tariffs
item.description: The House has finally rebuked President Trump over his unilateral trade war.

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth managementâs 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Bank Risk
item.description: AI is exposing the limits of legacy bank controls, making governance quality critical to resilience and investor confidence. The post AI Is Reshaping ...

item.title: What Makes an Ideal Leveraged Buyout Candidate?
item.description: In competitive PE markets, successful LBOs depend on durable business models, recurring cash flows, defensible market positions, and disciplined targe...

item.title: Rethinking Household Asset Allocation Under Capital Constraints
item.description: Leveraged ETFs arenât about more risk, theyâre about using less capital to achieve a desired risk profile in household portfolios. The post Rethinking...

item.title: Stockholmâs Capital Markets Success: More Than Meatballs
item.description: Stockholmâs capital-market success reflects more than IPOs and PE activity. It offers a case study in how culture, institutions, and incentives shape ...
